document: The ingestion pipeline extracts text from uploaded files, splits it into overlapping chunks, extracts structural metadata such as phase numbers, embeds each chunk, and upserts the vectors into a Qdrant collection. Identical content is deduplicated by hash. Every upload is registered in PostgreSQL with a content hash and chunk count.

The security layer uses OAuth2 password login that issues JWT tokens, with rate limiting on login and chat. Role-based access control defines three roles: viewer, engineer, and admin. Every privileged action is written to an audit trail, and accounts can be disabled without deleting their history.
key_points: rate limiting on login and chat
